page.title: Calculate My Hours — Compute Total, Business, Weekend & Weekday Hours from a Spreadsheet

meta.description: Upload a spreadsheet with start/end timestamps and get total hours, business hours, weekend hours, and weekday hours calculated to the minute. Free, runs entirely in your browser.
meta.keywords: hours calculator, business hours calculator, weekend hours calculator, weekday hours calculator, time between dates calculator, excel hours between two dates, timesheet calculator, payroll hours calculator, elapsed time calculator excel
meta.og_title: Calculate My Hours — Compute Total, Business, Weekend & Weekday Hours from a Spreadsheet
meta.og_description: Upload a spreadsheet with start/end timestamps and get total, business, weekend, and weekday hours calculated to the minute and returned in an updated file. Free, runs entirely in your browser.

content.headline: Easy Excel Timespan Calculator
content.subheadline: A Better Solution to NETWORKDAYS and MOD Formulas
content.description: Upload a spreadsheet of start and end timestamps with a header row included, choose one or more ways to measure the time between them, and download the result with new columns added — each calculated from exact timestamps down to the minute and reported to two decimal places. All processing happens locally in your browser; nothing is uploaded to a server.

metric.name: Total Hours
metric.description: Counts all elapsed hours between the start and end timestamp, with no exclusions.

metric.name: Business Hours
metric.description: Counts hours on non-weekend days only, within the configurable time window.

metric.name: Weekend Hours
metric.description: Counts hours that fall on days marked as weekend, from midnight to midnight.

metric.name: Weekday Hours
metric.description: Total Hours minus Weekend Hours — all hours on non-weekend days, full 24-hour span.

features.feature: Results appended as new columns to original data
All processing runs locally in your browser — nothing uploaded to a server
Results accurate to the minute, reported to two decimal places

content.cta: Drop your spreadsheet here or choose a file to get started.

page.title: About — Calculate My Hours | Easy Excel Timespan Calculator

meta.description: Learn how Calculate My Hours works — a free, browser-based tool that computes total, business, weekend, and weekday hours from spreadsheet timestamps.
meta.og_description: A free, browser-based tool that computes total, business, weekend, and weekday hours from spreadsheet timestamps. Nothing is uploaded to a server.

content.headline: Built for people who live in spreadsheets
content.intro: Calculate My Hours solves a specific, frustrating problem: you have a spreadsheet full of start and end timestamps, and you need to know how many hours fall in between — broken down by total, business, weekend, and weekday. No formulas. No NETWORKDAYS headaches. Just upload, configure, and download.

metric.name: Total Hours
metric.description: All elapsed hours between start and end, down to the minute. No exclusions.

metric.name: Business Hours
metric.description: Hours on non-weekend days within a configurable daily window — e.g. 9 AM to 5 PM.

metric.name: Weekend Hours
metric.description: Hours that fall on days you mark as weekend, midnight to midnight.

metric.name: Weekday Hours
metric.description: Total hours minus weekend hours — the full 24-hour span on non-weekend days.

section.title: How it works
section.p: Upload a spreadsheet (.xlsx, .xls, .ods, or .csv) with at least one column of start timestamps and one of end timestamps, with a header row at the top. The tool reads your file, lets you map which columns to use, and computes each selected metric row by row — calculating exact minute-level overlap with the relevant time windows.
Results are appended as new columns to your original data and returned as a downloadable .xlsx file. Your existing data is untouched; only the new columns are added.
You can select any combination of the four metrics, configure which days count as the weekend (Friday, Saturday, and/or Sunday), and set the daily start and end time for business hours calculations.

section.title: Your data stays on your computer
section.p: Everything runs locally in your browser using the SheetJS library. When you upload a file, it is read directly into memory on your device — it is never transmitted to a server, never stored in a database, and never leaves your machine. Closing the tab clears everything.

section.title: Why we built it
section.p: Spreadsheet formulas for time math — NETWORKDAYS, MOD, custom VBA — are brittle, hard to audit, and require rebuilding from scratch for every new file. We wanted a reusable tool you could hand to anyone on a team: upload your file, pick your columns, get your answer. No Excel expertise required.
The tool is free, supported by advertising, and will stay that way.

page.title: Contact — Calculate My Hours | Easy Excel Timespan Calculator

meta.description: Get in touch with the Calculate My Hours team. Report a bug, suggest a feature, or ask a question about the free spreadsheet hours calculator.
meta.og_description: Get in touch with the Calculate My Hours team. Report a bug, suggest a feature, or ask a question.

content.headline: We'd love to hear from you
content.intro: Have a bug to report, a feature to suggest, or a question about how the calculator works? Send us a note — we read everything.

form.help_text: The more detail, the faster we can help. For bugs, include the file type (.xlsx, .csv, etc.) and a description of the timestamps if you can.
form.success_message: Message sent! Thanks for reaching out. We'll get back to you as soon as we can.

content.privacy_note: This tool runs entirely in your browser — your spreadsheet data is never sent to us or stored anywhere. Messages sent through this form contain only what you type here.
